reference: I have a relative who is six years old. His eye problem, as per the report, includes computed tomography scan findings of mild left-sided proptosis with poorly circumscribed masses of increased density in the intraconal space of the left orbit, resulting in compression of the optic nerve. There are no obvious foci of calcification, suggestive of orbital lymphangioma. Please suggest management and treatment available in your institution.
text: I have a relative who is six years old. The eye problem, as per the report, includes computed tomography scan findings of mild left-sided cleft palate with poorly circumscribed mass of increased density in the infraconal face of the left orbit, resulting in compression of the optic nerve. There are no obvious cleft palate complications, suggestive of orbital lymphoma. Please suggest management and equipment available in your institution.
